Tag Archive

Stories Tagged "Greek Myth"

A focused archive path for Greek Myth, collecting 14 related Kyunolab stories across 2 shelfves.

Myths

Arachne Myth: Athena, Weaving, and the Origin of the Spider

A source-aware Kyunolab record tracing Arachne, Athena, the weaving contest, and the spider transformation.

Myths - 9 min read
Myths

Icarus Flight Myth: Wax Wings, the Sun, and the Warning About Flying Too High

Icarus is the Greek mythic figure who escapes Crete with wings made by Daedalus, but flies too close to the sun. The wax melts, the wings fail, and Icarus falls into the sea.

Myths - 9 min read
Myths

Icarus Myth: Wings, Warning, and the Meaning of Flying Too Close to the Sun

A unified-policy Kyunolab reading of Icarus Myth, based on existing external tradition rather than newly invented archive material.

Myths - 8 min read
Myths

Medusa and Perseus: The Gorgon, the Mirrored Shield, and the Power of the Severed Head

A source-aware Kyunolab article tracing Medusa, Perseus, the Gorgon sisters, the mirrored shield, and the strange afterlife of the severed head.

Myths - 9 min read
Legendary Places

Mount Olympus in Greek Myth: Home of the Gods and the Place Above the Human World

Mount Olympus is both a real mountain in Greece and the mythic home of the Olympian gods. In Greek tradition, it becomes the place above human affairs where divine power gathers.

Legendary Places - 8 min read
Myths

Narcissus and Echo: Reflection, Voice, and the Greek Myth of Unreachable Love

Narcissus and Echo is a Greek myth about two forms of unreachable love. Echo can only repeat the words of others, while Narcissus becomes trapped by his own reflection and cannot reach the image he desires.

Myths - 9 min read
Myths

Pandora's Jar: The Greek Myth Behind Pandora's Box

A remade archive reading of Pandora's jar, the later box tradition, and the question of why hope was left inside.

Myths - 9 min read
Myths

Persephone and the Underworld: The Greek Myth Behind Loss, Return, and the Seasons

The Persephone myth tells how Persephone is taken to the underworld, how Demeter grieves, and how Persephone returns for part of the year. The cycle is often connected with seasonal change.

Myths - 9 min read
Myths

Phaethon Sun Chariot Myth: Fire, Fall, and the Danger of Driving the Sun

Phaethon is a Greek mythic figure who asks to drive the chariot of the sun and loses control. The burning path threatens the world until Zeus strikes him down.

Myths - 9 min read
Myths

Prometheus Fire Myth: Theft, Punishment, and the Gift That Changed Humanity

Prometheus is a Greek mythic figure remembered for stealing fire and giving it to humanity. Zeus punishes him for crossing divine limits, often by binding him while an eagle tears at him.

Myths - 8 min read
Myths

Theseus and the Minotaur: Labyrinth, Thread, and the Monster at the Center

A unified-policy Kyunolab reading of Theseus and the Minotaur, based on existing external tradition rather than newly invented archive material.

Myths - 9 min read
Myths

Orpheus and Eurydice: Myth, Meaning, and the Rule Against Looking Back

A source-aware Kyunolab record tracing the underworld journey where love, music, a command, and one backward glance become the whole tragedy.

Myths - 10 min read
Myths

Persephone Myth: Underworld, Seasons, and the Meaning of Return

A source-aware Kyunolab record tracing the Greek myth that links descent, return, grief, agriculture, and the changing year.

Myths - 9 min read
Myths

Prometheus Myth: Fire, Punishment, and the Meaning of a Culture Hero

A source-aware Kyunolab record tracing the Greek myth of stolen fire, divine punishment, and the dangerous gift that changes human life.

Myths - 8 min read

Kyunolab Mystery Archive is a quiet story publication by Kyuno Lab, dedicated to legends, folklore, mysteries, and strange tales from the edges of memory.
